heading: Eligibility for grants
label: 1067g Eligibility for grants
notes: CodificationSection was formerly classified to prior to renumbering by .section 1135d of this titlePub. L. 105–244

(4)(A). , substituted “public and private nonprofit institutions of higher education” for “institutions of higher education”.Pub. L. 110–315, § 315(b)(2)(A)Par. (4)(C). , inserted “, the Department of Defense, or the National Institutes of Health” before the semicolon.Pub. L. 110–315, § 315(b)(2)(B)Par. (4)(D). , added subpar. L. 105–244, § 307(b)“(1) public and private nonprofit institutions that are minority institutions (as defined in ); andsection 1067k(3) of this title“(2) nonprofit science-oriented organizations, professional scientific societies, and all nonprofit, accredited colleges and universities which provide a needed service to a group of eligible minority institutions or which provide in-service training for project directors, scientists, and engineers from eligible minority institutions.”Par. (3) which read as follows: “for the purposes of , public and private nonprofit institutions that have at least 10 percent minority enrollment.”Pub. children.label: i
children.text: have a curriculum that includes science or engineering subjects; and

children.label: ii
children.text: enter into a partnership with public or private nonprofit institutions of higher education that award baccalaureate degrees in science and engineering;

children.label: A
children.text: provide a needed service to a group of minority institutions; or

children.label: B
children.text: provide in-service training for project directors, scientists, and engineers from minority institutions;

children.label: A
children.text: public and private nonprofit institutions of higher education which have a curriculum in science or engineering;

children.label: B
children.text: institutions of higher education that have a graduate or professional program in science or engineering;

children.label: C
children.text: research laboratories of, or under contract with, the Department of Energy, the Department of Defense, or the National Institutes of Health;

children.label: D
children.text: relevant offices of the National Aeronautics and Space Administration, National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration, National Science Foundation, and National Institute of Standards and Technology;

children.label: E
children.text: quasi-governmental entities that have a significant scientific or engineering mission; or

children.label: F
children.text: institutions of higher education that have State-sponsored centers for research in science, technology, engineering, and mathematics; or

children.label: A
children.text: at least one institution of higher education eligible for assistance under this subchapter or subchapter V;

children.label: B
children.text: at least one high-need local educational agency (as defined in ); andsection 1021 of this title

children.label: C
children.text: at least two community organizations or entities, such as businesses, professional associations, community-based organizations, philanthropic organizations, or State agencies.
